    "why_this_trip": [
      "Samoa is one of the most authentically Polynesian cultures still functioning on community terms",
      "Churches define the landscape — every village has at least one and community pride shows in everything",
      "Upolu and Savai'i together give a complete picture — connected by short ferry crossing",
      "The road reveals what beach postcards never show: a living, colourful, deeply social country"
    ],
    "who_this_is_for": [
      "Travellers who want Pacific culture beyond resorts and snorkelling",
      "Road trippers looking for an island circuit with genuine character at every stop",
      "Anyone combining Samoa with Tuvalu, Tonga or other Pacific island nations"
    ],
    "what_you_get": [
      "A road trip across Upolu with stops at coastal villages, waterfalls and traditional fale stays",
      "Savai'i island — less visited, bigger, and more rural than Upolu",
      "Church architecture on every corner — a visual guide to community identity",
      "A Pacific travel experience far from the tourist infrastructure of Bora Bora or Fiji"
    ],
    "difficulty_at_a_glance": [
      "Easy: paved roads, clear signage and welcoming communities",
      "Driving on the right-hand side of the road (they switched in 2009)",
      "Sunday is almost entirely closed — plan any Savai'i ferry crossing around this"
    ],
    "not_suitable": [
      "Travellers who need beach club infrastructure or 5-star resorts throughout",
      "Anyone who needs to rush — Samoa moves slowly by design"
    ]

      "Community-run guesthouses give more context than any hotel",
      "Ask in each village before photographing — courtesy is always appreciated and usually rewarded"
    ],

      "Assuming the ferry runs on Sunday — it may not, and your entire schedule can shift",
      "Spending all time near Apia instead of driving the full circuit",
      "Overlooking Savai'i — it is less visited and arguably more authentic than Upolu"
